serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately one cup or bowl of pistachio paste dessert, weighing around 150 grams, as typically served at a Chinese dessert shop.

nutrient_source.title: Pistachio butter (nut paste), USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy — NDB 12652 / FDC ID 170184
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct entry for 'Intense Pistachio Paste' dessert exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or ICMR-NIN. USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for pistachio butter/paste (FDC 170184) is used as the closest approved analogue. Values represent a pure pistachio paste base. The dessert version served at RUXU may include added sugar and dairy, which would increase energy and carbohydrate content; values here reflect the pistachio paste component per 100 g. Sodium is adjusted upward slightly to reflect a prepared dessert context. Confidence is medium due to this adaptation.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for pistachio paste dessert. Pistachios have a published GI of approximately 15 (low) per the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 international GI tables. Pistachio paste is predominantly fat and protein with moderate low-GI carbohydrate, consistent with a GI of ~15. Glycemic load per 150 g serving calculated as: (15 × 27.5 × 1.5) / 100 = 6.2 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium content (~6 mg per 100 g) and high potassium (~1025 mg per 100 g)
hypertension.guidance: Pistachio paste is naturally very low in sodium and rich in potassium, making it a favourable choice for individuals managing hypertension; however, portion size should be monitored due to high caloric density, and any added salt in the prepared dessert version should be confirmed with the vendor.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index (~15) but high caloric density and moderate carbohydrate content with possible added sugar in the dessert preparation
type2_diabetes.guidance: The pistachio paste base has a low GI of approximately 15 and is rich in dietary fibre (10.3 g/100 g) and healthy fats that attenuate postprandial glucose response; however, individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should be cautious of added sugars and the high energy density (~567 kcal/100 g) in the restaurant-prepared version, and should limit portion size to approximately 100–150 g per serving.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High monounsaturated and polyunsaturated fat content with low saturated fat and zero cholesterol
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Pistachio paste provides a favourable fatty acid profile dominated by monounsaturated (23.3 g/100 g) and polyunsaturated fats (13.5 g/100 g) with low saturated fat (5.6 g/100 g) and zero cholesterol, consistent with cardiovascular-protective dietary patterns; moderate consumption as part of a balanced diet is appropriate for individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Intense Pistachio Paste (浓郁开心果酱) is a rich, smooth nut-based dessert paste made from premium California Kerman pistachios ground to a velvety consistency. It is served at RUXU 入续 糖水铺与包, a Chinese dessert shop in Singapore's Funan mall, as part of a contemporary Chinese dessert (糖水) menu. The paste delivers an intensely aromatic pistachio flavour with a creamy, dense texture, topped with crushed pistachio kernels for added crunch and visual appeal. Nutritionally, pistachio paste is calorie-dense (~567 kcal per 100 g) and rich in healthy unsaturated fats, plant-based protein (~20 g/100 g), dietary fibre (~10 g/100 g), potassium, magnesium, phosphorus, and B vitamins including B6 and thiamine. It has a low glycemic index (~15) due to its high fat, protein, and fibre content, making it one of the lower-GI nut pastes available. The dish is naturally very low in sodium and contains no cholesterol. Clinically, it is suitable in moderate portions for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease, while those with Type 2 Diabetes should monitor portion size and confirm added sugar content in the restaurant preparation. The dish is vegetarian and gluten-free but likely contains dairy in the restaurant version. It is priced at SGD 11.20 per serving.
common_user_queries: Is pistachio paste good for diabetics?

confidence_reason: No direct nutritional entry for 'Intense Pistachio Paste' as a prepared dessert exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, or FAO/INFOODS SEA databases. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy pistachio butter/paste (FDC 170184), which represents the pure pistachio paste component. The restaurant preparation may include added sugar, cream, or dairy that would alter the macronutrient profile. GI is estimated from published pistachio GI data (University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. 2008). Confidence is medium due to source adaptation and unknown restaurant-specific formulation.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy FDC 170184 (pistachio butter/paste) as no direct approved-source entry for this prepared dessert exists. The restaurant version may contain added sugar and dairy, increasing energy and carbohydrate values beyond those reported here. 2. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central for pistachio paste; pistachios contain no industrially produced trans fats. 3. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 — confirmed zero as this is a plant-based nut product containing no animal-derived B12 per USDA FoodData Central. 4. vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central; pistachios contain no vitamin D. 5. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero; pistachios are a plant food with no cholesterol. 6. Sodium value of 6.0 mg/100 g reflects the natural sodium content of pistachio paste per USDA; the restaurant preparation may have higher sodium if salt is added. 7. GI of 15 is an analogous estimate based on published pistachio GI data from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 international GI tables. 8. Multilingual names provided for Singaporean cuisine context (English, Chinese Simplified, Chinese Traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) per G6 rules. 9. FoodOn match FOODON_03302757 and FoodEx2 A00LC are closest_parent matches; no exact ontology code exists for pistachio paste dessert. 10. Dietary flag is_vegan set to false and is_dairy_free set to false as the restaurant preparation likely includes cream or dairy; is_vegetarian set to true. is_halal set to true as pistachios are inherently halal and no pork/alcohol ingredients are indicated.
